lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਆਖਣਿ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਆਖੈ ਇਕ ਦੂ ਇਕੁ ਸਿਆਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: naanak aakhan sabh ko aakhai ik doo ik siaanaa |
lines.english: O Nanak, everyone speaks of Him, each one wiser than the rest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਵਡੀ ਨਾਈ ਕੀਤਾ ਜਾ ਕਾ ਹੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: vaddaa saahib vaddee naaee keetaa jaa kaa hovai |
lines.english: Great is the Master, Great is His Name. Whatever happens is according to His Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜੇ ਕੋ ਆਪੌ ਜਾਣੈ ਅਗੈ ਗਇਆ ਨ ਸੋਹੈ ॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: naanak je ko aapau jaanai agai geaa na sohai |21|
lines.english: O Nanak, one who claims to know everything shall not be decorated in the world hereafter. ||21||
There are nether worlds beneath nether worlds, and hundreds of thousands of heavenly worlds above.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਸ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਕਹਨਿ ਕਤੇਬਾ ਅਸੁਲੂ ਇਕੁ ਧਾਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: sehas atthaarah kehan katebaa asuloo ik dhaat |
lines.english: The scriptures say that there are 18,000 worlds, but in reality, there is only One Universe.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓੜਕ ਓੜਕ ਭਾਲਿ ਥਕੇ ਵੇਦ ਕਹਨਿ ਇਕ ਵਾਤ ॥
lines.translit: orrak orrak bhaal thake ved kehan ik vaat |
lines.english: The Vedas say that you can search and search for them all, until you grow weary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੇਖਾ ਹੋਇ ਤ ਲਿਖੀਐ ਲੇਖੈ ਹੋਇ ਵਿਣਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: lekhaa hoe ta likheeai lekhai hoe vinaas |
lines.english: If you try to write an account of this, you will surely finish yourself before you finish writing it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਵਡਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਆਪੁ ॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: naanak vaddaa aakheeai aape jaanai aap |22|
lines.english: O Nanak, call Him Great! He Himself knows Himself. ||22||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦੀਆ ਅਤੈ ਵਾਹ ਪਵਹਿ ਸਮੁੰਦਿ ਨ ਜਾਣੀਅਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: nadeea atai vaah paveh samund na jaaneeeh |
lines.english: the streams and rivers flowing into the ocean do not know its vastness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਸਾਲਾਹਿ ਏਤੀ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਨ ਪਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: saalaahee saalaeh etee surat na paaeea |
lines.english: The praisers praise the Lord, but they do not obtain intuitive understanding

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਮੁੰਦ ਸਾਹ ਸੁਲਤਾਨ ਗਿਰਹਾ ਸੇਤੀ ਮਾਲੁ ਧਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: samund saah sulataan girahaa setee maal dhan |
lines.english: Even kings and emperors, with mountains of property and oceans of wealth

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀੜੀ ਤੁਲਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਨੀ ਜੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਨਹੁ ਨ ਵੀਸਰਹਿ ॥੨੩॥
lines.translit: keerree tul na hovanee je tis manahu na veesareh |23|
lines.english: -these are not even equal to an ant, who does not forget God. ||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਸਿਫਤੀ ਕਹਣਿ ਨ ਅੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: ant na sifatee kehan na ant |
lines.english: Endless are His Praises, endless are those who speak them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਜਾਪੈ ਕਿਆ ਮਨਿ ਮੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: ant na jaapai kiaa man mant |
lines.english: His limits cannot be perceived. What is the Mystery of His Mind?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਕਰਣੈ ਦੇਣਿ ਨ ਅੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: ant na karanai den na ant |
lines.english: Endless are His Actions, endless are His Gifts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਵੇਖਣਿ ਸੁਣਣਿ ਨ ਅੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: ant na vekhan sunan na ant |
lines.english: Endless is His Vision, endless is His Hearing.
The limits of the created universe cannot be perceived.
Its limits here and beyond cannot be perceived.
Many struggle to know His limits,
Great is the Master, High is His Heavenly Home.
The more you say about them, the more there still remains to be said.
No one can know these limits.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੇ ਅੰਤ ਨ ਪਾਏ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: taa ke ant na paae jaeh |
lines.english: but His limits cannot be found.
Only one as Great and as High as God
Only He Himself is that Great. He Himself knows Himself.
Highest of the High, above all is His Name.
can know His Lofty and Exalted State.
The Great Giver does not hold back anything.
O Nanak, by His Glance of Grace, He bestows His Blessings. ||24||
His Blessings are so abundant that there can be no written account of them.
There are so many great, heroic warriors begging at the Door of the Infinite Lord.
So many contemplate and dwell upon Him, that they cannot be counted.
So many take and take again, and then deny receiving.
So many waste away to death engaged in corruption.
Liberation from bondage comes only by Your Will.
So many foolish consumers keep on consuming.
Even these are Your Gifts, O Great Giver!
So many endure distress, deprivation and constant abuse.
No one else has any say in this.
If some fool should presume to say that he does,
he shall learn, and feel the effects of his folly.
He Himself knows, He Himself gives.
O Nanak, is the king of kings. ||25||
One who is blessed to sing the Praises of the Lord,
Few, very few are those who acknowledge this.
Priceless are His Dealers, Priceless are His Treasures.
Priceless are His Virtues, Priceless are His Dealings.
Priceless are those who come to Him, Priceless are those who buy from Him.
Priceless is the Divine Law of Dharma, Priceless is the Divine Court of Justice.
Priceless are His Blessings, Priceless is His Banner and Insignia.
Priceless are the scales, priceless are the weights.
Priceless is Love for Him, Priceless is absorption into Him.
Priceless is His Mercy, Priceless is His Royal Command.
Priceless, O Priceless beyond expression!
Speak of Him continually, and remain absorbed in His Love.
The Vedas and the Puraanas speak.
The scholars speak and lecture.
Brahma speaks, Indra speaks.
